metadata.title: Humanitarians AI - An Educational Sandbox
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown
metadata.credit_line: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The programmer is still employed, but the wage premium once attached to that skill is gone. The sandbox integrates diverse technical projects into a single seamless narrative framework. The RAMAN Effect is an open-source platform advancing public-health wastewater epidemiology using Surface-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy. A modern research university is not a monolith, and some legal departments treat any subjective assessment as litigation risk.

artifactLines: The programmer is still employed, but the wage premium once attached to that skill is gone.
The sandbox integrates diverse technical projects into a single seamless narrative framework.
The RAMAN Effect is an open-source platform advancing public-health wastewater epidemiology using Surface-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy.
A modern research university is not a monolith, and some legal departments treat any subjective assessment as litigation risk.

beats.narration_text: The tension that the programmer is still employed but the premium is gone, set against RAMAN using SERS for wastewater-based epidemiology and legal departments treating subjective assessment as litigation risk, shows me the sandbox spans real projects. I want to see how they cohere. Can you (1) explain how a research university being non-monolithic enables a program like this, (2) sketch how the RAMAN Effect turns wastewater signals into public-health insight via Surface-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy, and (3) describe how diverse technical projects get integrated into one narrative framework without feeling forced?

output: Now the same job title requires a medical degree or legal credent

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'Humanitarians AI - An Educational Sandbox' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'Humanitarians AI - An Educational Sandbox' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
